tasks.description: Update the graph renderer so dense commits show a stable priority-ordered subset of refs plus a grouped-overflow badge instead of an unbounded inline sequence.
steps: Manual app check in pnpm tauri dev confirms sparse commits still render normal badges
Manual app check confirms dense commits keep one-row readability with grouped overflow

tasks.description: Keep hitTest() synchronized with the new visible overlay model so ref and row targeting stay trustworthy after overflow grouping.
steps: Manual app check confirms visible badges remain targetable
Manual app check confirms hidden overflow refs are not individually targetable at stale positions

tasks.description: Extend existing graph smoke-style validation with dense-ref scenarios that prove ordering, overflow, and rendering contracts remain stable.
steps: Focused assertions cover dense commits with multiple local branches, remote branches, tags, and Head

tasks.description: Prove the refined overlays work in the production /repo/history route without regressing graph-detail binding from bd-145.1.
steps: Manual app check in pnpm tauri dev confirms overlay readability in /repo/history and no regression in commit selection/detail sync

patterns: Badge width: estimateRefBadgeWidth() shared between render and hitTest
Ref styles: getRefStyle() encodes visual language per ref type
nonGoals: New graph routes or layout algorithms
DOM/SVG overlay layers or tooltip systems
New backend IPC commands or RefData shape changes
Ref-click actions, filtering, pinning, or hiding interactions
